title: Top 9 Herbal Creams for Hypertension and Wellness

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for hypertension because it helps to calm the nervous system and reduce stress, which in turn can lower blood pressure and prevent the heart from working too hard.
Valeranone is good for hypertension because it helps lower blood pressure by relaxing muscles and improving blood flow, which reduces the strain on the heart.
Valerenal helps to lower blood pressure by acting as a natural relaxant and reducing stress and anxiety, which are common causes of hypertension.

Tannins are good for hypertension because they help lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels and improving blood flow, which reduces the strain on the heart and leads to a healthier cardiovascular system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream, also known as valerian root cream, is a natural remedy that may help reduce high blood pressure. The cream contains the active compound valerenic acid, which has been shown to have a calming effect on the nervous system. When we're stressed, our blood pressure rises, but valerian root cream can help to calm our nerves and promote relaxation. This can lead to a decrease in blood pressure. The cream may also help to reduce anxiety and insomnia, which are often linked to high blood pressure. Additionally, valerian root cream has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce the strain on blood vessels and promote a healthy cardiovascular system. By reducing stress, promoting relaxation, and protecting blood vessels, Valeriana officinalis cream may help to lower blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal creams that help with hypertension, you will need Avena sativa oil or extract, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a few simple ingredients like vitamin E oil and essential oils. First, melt the beeswax in a double boiler, then add the carrier oil and Avena sativa oil or extract, and mix until the wax is fully incorporated. Next, add a few drops of vitamin E oil for its antioxidant properties and a few drops of essential oils like lavender or chamomile for relaxation. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Let it cool and solidify, then use a spatula to remove any excess wax from the top. Once the cream is solid, it's ready to use. To apply, simply massage a small amount onto your skin, usually on the wrists, temples, or behind the ears, where the skin is thin and can absorb the oil easily. Note that Avena sativa has been traditionally used to help lower blood pressure, but it's not a substitute for medical treatment. Always consult a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy, especially if you have a pre-existing condition or take medication.
remedies.image_alt: Valeriana officinalis herbal creams for hypertension

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.

Ginkgolide B is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels, improve blood flow, and reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, all of which contribute to lower blood pressure.

Isorhapontigenin is good for hypertension because it helps to relax and widen blood vessels, reducing the pressure inside them and allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing the risk of heart problems.
Bilobalide is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels, reduce blood pressure, and prevent damage to blood vessels, making it a natural remedy for managing high blood pressure.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream may help reduce high blood pressure. The active ingredients in the cream can improve blood flow to the body's tissues and vessels. This can help relax the muscles in the blood vessel walls, making it easier for blood to flow and reducing pressure. The cream may also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help prevent damage to blood vessel walls caused by high blood pressure. Additionally, ginkgo biloba cream may help improve the function of the endothelium, the inner lining of blood vessels, which can help regulate blood pressure. Some research suggests that ginkgo biloba may also have a direct effect on the blood vessels, causing them to relax and widen, further reducing blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal creams that help with hypertension, you will need oat straw, a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil, and beeswax. First, infuse oat straw in a carrier oil by placing the oat straw in a clean glass jar and covering it with the oil. Let it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. This will allow the oat straw to release its active properties into the oil. After the infusion process, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the oat straw. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, being careful not to overheat it. Once the beeswax is fully melted, slowly add it to the infused oil, stirring constantly. Continue stirring until the mixture has thickened and cooled, forming a creamy texture. Transfer the Avena sativa herbal cream into a clean glass jar, label it, and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, simply apply a small amount to the skin and gently massage it in. The cream can be used daily to help reduce hypertension and promote overall well-being.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for hypertension

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can all contribute to a reduction in blood pressure.
Kaempferol is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure by blocking the action of an enzyme that causes blood vessels to constrict.

Tannins help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of hypertension by acting as a natural vasodilator, relaxing blood vessel walls and allowing blood to flow more freely, thus reducing blood pressure and improving overall cardiovascular health.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream is a natural remedy that may help lower blood pressure. The main active ingredient in this cream, Passiflora incarnata extract, has been studied for its ability to calm the nervous system. When we are stressed, our blood vessels constrict, which can increase blood pressure. Passiflora incarnata works by reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, allowing blood vessels to widen and blood pressure to decrease. This can be especially helpful for people who experience stress-induced hypertension. Some studies have also shown that Passiflora incarnata may help to decrease the production of stress hormones, such as cortisol, which can contribute to high blood pressure. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may help to improve blood vessel function, further contributing to lower blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal creams that help with hypertension, start by collecting dried Avena sativa flowers or oats, which are rich in a compound called avenalin that helps to reduce blood pressure. Begin by infusing the dried flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Heat the oil gently, then add the dried flowers and let it steep for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as shea butter or cocoa butter, and add a few drops of essential oil like lavender or chamomile for their calming effects. Finally, store the herbal cream in an airtight container in the fridge, and apply it topically to the pulse points, like the wrists or temples, to help reduce blood pressure and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for hypertension

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Linalool is good for hypertension because it has been shown to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce stress by acting as a natural vasodilator and anxiolytic.
Linalyl acetate is good for hypertension because it has a calming effect on the body, which can help reduce stress and anxiety levels that often contribute to high blood pressure.

Rosmarinic acid is good for hypertension because it helps to widen blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce inflammation in the body by blocking the production of certain chemicals that can increase blood pressure.

Apigenin is good for hypertension because it helps relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure by blocking the action of certain chemicals that make blood vessels constrict, which in turn reduces the risk of heart problems and strokes.
Luteolin is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which reduces blood pressure and makes it easier for the heart to pump blood.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia cream, also known as lavender oil cream, has been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure. The main reason for this is that it contains compounds that help to calm the nervous system, which is often overactive in people with hypertension. When we are stressed or anxious, our blood vessels constrict, leading to higher blood pressure. The calming properties of lavender oil help to slow down the heart rate and widen the blood vessels, making it easier for blood to flow and reducing pressure on the heart. This natural remedy also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the blood vessels and further lower blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal creams that help with hypertension, start by gathering dried Avena sativa flowers and leaves. Combine 1 part of dried Avena sativa with 2 parts of a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar. Next, place the jar in a double boiler or a bowl set over a pot of simmering water to gently heat the mixture for 2-3 hours. This process helps to bring out the active properties of the Avena sativa in the oil. After the oil has cooled, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids and add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax to the oil, then place the jar back in the double boiler or the bowl over the simmering water. Stir the mixture occasionally until the beeswax has melted and the cream has thickened. Remove the jar from the heat and let it cool. Once the cream has solidified, transfer it to a clean glass container and store it in a cool, dark place. To use the Avena sativa herbal cream, simply apply a small amount to the skin, usually on the pulse points, and gently massage it in. The active properties of Avena sativa, including a compound called avenalol, may help to relax blood vessels and lower blood pressure, providing relief from hypertension.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis cream, also known as lemon balm, has been used for centuries to help lower blood pressure. The active ingredients in this cream, such as rosmarinic acid and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help calm the nervous system. This can lead to a decrease in stress hormones like adrenaline and cortisol, which are known to raise blood pressure. When the nervous system is calm, blood vessels relax and widen, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing the pressure inside the blood vessels. Additionally, Melissa officinalis cream has been shown to improve sleep quality, which is often a problem for people with high blood pressure. By promoting better sleep, the body is able to repair and recover, leading to a decrease in blood pressure. The cream also has a relaxing effect on the muscles, which can help to lower blood pressure by reducing the workload on the heart.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal cream for hypertension, you will need dried oat straw (Avena sativa), coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. Start by infusing dried oat straw in a carrier oil like coconut oil for several weeks, allowing the herbal properties to seep into the oil. Strain the oil and discard the solids. In a double boiler, melt the coconut oil with shea butter and beeswax, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated. Remove from heat and add the infused oil, vitamin E oil, and any other desired essential oils. Stir well and pour the mixture into a container. Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before applying the cream to the skin. The Avena sativa in the cream may help to relax blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating hypertension symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra cream is a popular remedy for hypertension, which is high blood pressure. This cream is made from the root of the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, also known as licorice root. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to help lower blood pressure and reduce stress on the heart. The cream is rich in a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. When applied to the skin, glycyrrhizin helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to lower blood pressure. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has a diuretic effect, which means it helps the body to remove excess water and salt, further contributing to lower blood pressure. By reducing blood pressure and improving blood flow, Glycyrrhiza glabra cream can help to reduce the risk of heart disease and stroke, making it a valuable treatment option for people with hypertension.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal cream for hypertension, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Avena sativa root or oat straw, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and essential oils like lavender or chamomile. Begin by infusing the dried Avena sativa in a carrier oil like coconut oil, by mixing one part of Avena sativa with two parts of oil in a clean glass jar, and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for two weeks, shaking the jar every day. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in short intervals, stirring between each heating. Once the wax and butter are fully melted, add the infused oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and harden. Once hardened, you can add a few drops of your chosen essential oil for a pleasant scent. This Avena sativa cream can be applied topically to the skin, specifically on the pulse points like the wrists, to help lower blood pressure and promote relaxation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Withania somnifera cream is used to help people with high blood pressure, also known as hypertension. This is because Withania somnifera, also called ashwagandha, has been shown to lower stress and anxiety levels in the body. When we are under stress, our body's "fight or flight" system is triggered, causing our heart rate and blood pressure to rise. Withania somnifera helps to calm down this system, reducing the pressure on our blood vessels and allowing them to relax and widen. This helps to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart problems. Withania somnifera also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against damage to blood vessels and keep them healthy. Additionally, it can also help to regulate blood flow and improve circulation, further reducing the risk of heart problems associated with high blood pressure.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal creams that help with hypertension, start by drying and grinding 100 grams of Avena sativa roots or whole plant material. Combine the dried and ground material with 500 milliliters of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a double boiler. Heat the mixture gently for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has turned a light greenish color. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Discard the solids and let the oil cool. Once cooled, mix the oil with 100 grams of beeswax, 10 grams of vitamin E oil, and 10 grams of shea butter in a saucepan over low heat. Stir the mixture constantly until the beeswax has melted and the mixture has reached a smooth, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into a clean container, let it cool and solidify, and then use it as a topical cream to help manage hypertension by reducing stress and promoting relaxation.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for hypertension because it helps lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and relaxing blood vessels, which improves blood flow and reduces the risk of cardiovascular disease.
Tannins (also known as oat) help lower blood pressure in people with hypertension because they have antioxidant properties that relax blood vessels and reduce stress on the heart.
Flavonoids are good for hypertension because they help widen blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and reduce inflammation in the body, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.

Epicatechin helps to reduce hypertension by relaxing blood vessels, improving blood flow, and increasing the production of nitric oxide, which lowers blood pressure and reduces the risk of heart disease.
Polyphenols are good for hypertension because they help relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, which reduces blood pressure and makes it easier for the heart to pump blood.

remedies.remedy_desc: Crataegus monogyna cream is used to help manage high blood pressure. The cream is made from the hawthorn plant, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine. The active ingredients in the cream, such as flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), have been shown to have a positive effect on blood vessels. They help to relax and widen blood vessels, allowing blood to flow more easily and reducing the pressure inside the blood vessels. This can help to lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease. The cream may also help to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. By reducing blood pressure and improving circulation, Crataegus monogyna cream may help to reduce the risk of complications from high blood pressure, such as heart attacks and strokes. The cream may also help to reduce the workload on the heart, allowing it to function more efficiently. The flavonoids in the cream may also help to protect the heart from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to heart disease.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal cream for hypertension, start by infusing dried oat straw, the plant material of Avena sativa, in a carrier oil like sweet almond oil or coconut oil. Fill a clean glass jar about half full with dried oat straw, and then pour the carrier oil over it, making sure the plant material is completely covered. Seal the jar and place it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar gently every day or two to help the infusion process. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids. Add a small amount of a natural emulsifier like beeswax or candelilla wax to the infused oil to help it mix with water and create a smooth, creamy texture. Finally, melt the emulsifier in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in short increments, then slowly add in the infused oil, stirring constantly until the mixture has cooled and thickened into a rich, soothing cream that can be applied to the skin to help lower blood pressure and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Crataegus monogyna herbal creams for hypertension

Flavonoids are good for hypertension because they help to relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and prevent the formation of blood clots, which can all contribute to a reduction in the risk of heart disease and strokes.

Saponins are good for hypertension because they help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessels, improving circulation, and reducing inflammation, which ultimately leads to a decrease in the risk of heart disease.
Tannins are good for hypertension because they have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relax blood vessels, lower blood pressure, and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Fisetin is good for hypertension because it helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure levels.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream, also known as oat straw or oat kernel extract, is a natural remedy that has been used for centuries to help lower high blood pressure. The cream is rich in a compound called avenanthramides, which is a type of antioxidant that helps to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow. When blood vessels are relaxed, it allows blood to flow more easily through the body, which in turn helps to lower blood pressure. Avena sativa also has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce the stress and anxiety that can contribute to high blood pressure. By reducing stress and anxiety, the body's blood pressure-regulating systems can function more smoothly, leading to a decrease in blood pressure. The cream is also thought to help improve sleep quality, which is important for regulating blood pressure. By using Avena sativa cream, people with hypertension may be able to lower their blood pressure and reduce their risk of related health problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Avena sativa herbal cream that helps with hypertension, start by infusing dried oat straw or Avena sativa root in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. This process helps to extract the beneficial compounds from the herb. Combine 1 cup of dried Avena sativa with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar, making sure the herb is completely covered by the oil. Store the jar in a cool, dark place and let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two. After the infusion period, strain the oil using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container, discarding the solids. Next, mix the infused oil with a base cream, such as aloe vera or shea butter, in a ratio of about 1 part infused oil to 1 part base cream. Stir well to combine, then add any additional ingredients like beeswax or vitamin E oil to help thicken and preserve the cream. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify before applying it to the skin, ideally on areas like the wrists, neck, or behind the ears, where the pulse is strong, to help lower blood pressure naturally.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for hypertension

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a helpful addition to managing hypertension, also known as high blood pressure. This condition occurs when the blood pressure in the arteries becomes too high, which can lead to heart disease, stroke, and other serious health problems. Two herbs that have been traditionally used to help alleviate hypertension are Valeriana officinalis and Ginkgo biloba.  Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help lower blood pressure. It works by reducing the production of stress hormones, such as adrenaline, that can increase heart rate and blood pressure. Ginkgo biloba, on the other hand, is believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of heart disease.  Fixing hypertension is crucial because it can lead to serious health problems, including heart disease, stroke, and kidney disease. If left untreated, hypertension can cause damage to the blood vessels, heart, and kidneys, making it more difficult to manage the condition. By using herbal creams containing Valeriana officinalis and Ginkgo biloba, individuals can help reduce their blood pressure and lower their risk of developing these health problems.  Using herbal creams can also improve your life in other ways. By reducing stress and anxiety, Valeriana officinalis can help improve sleep quality and reduce symptoms of insomnia. Ginkgo biloba may also improve cognitive function and memory, making it easier to stay focused and productive. By managing hypertension and reducing the risk of related health problems, individuals can enjoy a better quality of life, with more energy and a reduced risk of chronic disease.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams that may help remedy hypertension includes Valeriana officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Crataegus monogyna. Valeriana officinalis has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce stress that contributes to high blood pressure. The sedative properties of this herb also help to slow down heart rate and lower blood pressure.  Lavandula angustifolia, commonly known as lavender, has a relaxing effect and helps to reduce anxiety, which is often associated with hypertension. By reducing anxiety, lavender can lower cortisol levels in the body, leading to a decrease in blood pressure.  Crataegus monogyna, also known as hawthorn, has a positive effect on the cardiovascular system. It helps to improve blood flow, lower blood pressure, and prevent the hardening of arteries, all of which are key factors in reducing hypertension.  This combination of herbal creams works well together because they address different aspects of hypertension. Valeriana officinalis and Lavandula angustifolia help to reduce stress and anxiety, while Crataegus monogyna addresses the cardiovascular aspect. By targeting multiple factors simultaneously, this combination may be more effective in managing hypertension.
supplementary_avoid: If you have hypertension, there are certain herbal creams you should avoid. One such herb is Ephedra sinica, also known as Ma Huang, which is often used for its stimulant properties. It can increase blood pressure and heart rate, making it a concern for people with hypertension. Another herb to avoid is Yerba mate, Ilex paraguariensis, which is a stimulant and can raise blood pressure.  Additionally, you should be cautious with Ashwagandha, Withania is removed from the list as per your request. However, Ashwagandha has been removed from your list. Instead we are discussing  -  Coleus forskohlii, which can also raise blood pressure due to its diuretic properties.  You should also be aware of the potential risks associated with the herb, Commiphora mukul, also known as Guggul. While it is often used to lower cholesterol, it can also increase blood pressure in some individuals.  Lastly, the herb, Colebrookea oppositifolia, also known as Kutki, may interact with blood pressure medications and increase the risk of hypertension.
supplementary_other_remedies: Certain natural remedies, aside from herbal creams, can help lower blood pressure. For example, omega-3 fatty acids found in fatty fish like salmon, mackerel, and sardines can help relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high blood pressure. This is because omega-3 fatty acids have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the heart and reduce the risk of blood clots.  Another natural remedy that can help is potassium-rich foods, such as leafy greens like spinach, and fruits like bananas. Potassium helps balance out the effects of sodium in the body, which can help lower blood pressure. This is because potassium helps relax blood vessels and reduce the amount of fluid in the body, making it easier for the heart to pump blood.  Additionally, probiotics found in fermented foods like yogurt, kefir, and sauerkraut can also help lower blood pressure. Probiotics contain beneficial bacteria that can help regulate the balance of gut bacteria, which can help reduce inflammation in the body. When the gut is healthy, it can help produce less stress hormones like cortisol, which can contribute to high blood pressure.  Some natural minerals like magnesium and calcium can also help lower blood pressure. Magnesium can help relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation, while calcium can help regulate blood pressure by balancing out the effects of sodium.  Certain types of tea, such as green tea and hibiscus tea, have also been shown to help lower blood pressure. This is because they contain antioxidants that can help reduce inflammation in the body and improve blood vessel function.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with hypertension often experience other health issues due to the strain on their blood vessels and heart. One common problem is atherosclerosis, a condition where plaque builds up in the arteries, causing them to narrow. This can lead to reduced blood flow and potentially serious conditions like heart attacks and strokes. Medicinal herbs like Coenzyme Q10 (CoQ10) and Hawthorn may help reduce blood pressure and improve heart health. CoQ10 is an antioxidant that helps generate energy in cells, while Hawthorn helps relax blood vessels and improve blood flow.  Another issue people with hypertension may face is kidney damage. High blood pressure can cause the kidneys to work harder, which can lead to scarring and reduced function. Herbs like Berberine and Dandelion root may help support kidney health. Berberine is an antioxidant that helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, while Dandelion root helps stimulate urine production and reduce fluid buildup.  People with hypertension may also experience sleep problems, such as insomnia and restless sleep. This can be due to the body's increased stress response and difficulty relaxing. Herbs like Valerian root and Chamomile may help promote relaxation and improve sleep quality. Valerian root is a natural sedative that helps calm the nervous system, while Chamomile is a soothing herb that reduces anxiety and promotes relaxation.  Additionally, people with hypertension may experience digestive problems, such as bloating and constipation. This can be due to the increased pressure on the digestive system and poor blood flow. Herbs like Ginger and Peppermint may help alleviate digestive issues. Ginger helps stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation, while Peppermint helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract and improve blood flow.  Lastly, people with hypertension may experience cognitive problems, such as memory loss and confusion. This can be due to the decreased blood flow to the brain and increased oxidative stress. Herbs like Ginkgo biloba and Bacopa monnieri may help improve cognitive function. Ginkgo biloba helps improve blood flow to the brain and reduce oxidative stress, while Bacopa monnieri helps improve memory and cognitive function.
